title: Upon Blanch.
content:
Blanch swears her husband's lovely; when a scald
    Has blear'd his eyes: besides, his head is bald
    Next, his wild ears, like leathern wings full spread,
    Flutter to fly, and bear away his head.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Blanch."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
